repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Windows followup of #35223



## Problem
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/35223#issuecomment-4143161388
`server_stop()` stops listening, but on Windows the named pipe is not immediately reusable. `uv_close()` is asynchronous, and the pipe name may remain unavailable until after the old server exits and the OS finishes releasing it. If the new server tries to restore the old `--listen` address too early, restart can fail.
## Solution
The new server now starts on a temporary
address and receives the original address via restart handoff.
It then retries `serverstart()` from startup until the canonical address becomes available and all ui's done migrating or grace period for migration is exceeded, then it closes the
temporary listener.

> **Known limitation on Windows:** `:restart +cmd` may run before the original `--listen` address has been reclaimed. Commands that read address may get the temporary one instead of original address.

comment_data.body: might as well retry on all platforms, it's harmless. but keep the comment about Windows.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -262,11 +262,15 @@ describe('TUI :restart', function()
     local server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
     local _, server_pid = server_session:request('nvim_call_function', 'getpid', {})
     local function assert_new_pid()
+      server_session:close()
       if is_os('win') then
-        return -- FIXME
+        -- On Windows, --listen address is restored async (after old server exits).
+        retry(nil, 5000, function()
+          server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)
+        end)
+      else
+        server_session = n.connect(server_pipe)

comment_data.body: rather than add another ui-event arg, can we somehow fixup things in the server-side? we were planning to do the `command` part here anyway, which may then give more flexibility in this server-side logic for deciding what `listen_addr` to send to the ui client.
I disagree. Having to retry on non-Windows is a regression.

comment_data.body:
Dropping the `NULL` check doesn't affect the value of `listen_arg`. It usually shouldn't be NULL anyway.
```suggestion
    if (di != NULL && di->di_tv.v_type == VAR_STRING) {
```

I'm not sure if stopping the new server's initial listening address is a good idea. It's sent in the "restart" UI event, while `UIEnter` is triggered when the first UI attaches. After #34367 is addressed there may be other UIs that haven't attached yet.

can we do this logic in Lua? if you add a function to `runtime/lua/vim/_core/server.lua` you can call it like this: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/df8d98173cbc03d78da18fee7f31f130db2c9e44/src/nvim/eval/funcs.c#L6334

is `nvim__set_restart_handoff` this actually needed, or could we call Lua here?

    rpc_send_call(..., "nvim_exec_lua", ...)

to avoid a big old Lua string here, it may be convenient to [define most of the logic in `_core/server.lua`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38539#discussion_r3075956511).
Not sure about these
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -34,4 +34,56 @@ function M.serverlist(listed)
   return found
 end
 
+-- Canonical --listen address persisted across chained restarts (Windows).
+M._restart_canonical_hint = nil ---@type string?
+
+--- Called on the new server via nvim_exec_lua RPC from the old server (:restart).
+--- Windows named pipes can't be rebound immediately, so the new server starts on a
+--- temporary bootstrap address and polls until the canonical address is reclaimable.
+--- @param canonical_addr string  The original --listen address to reclaim.
+--- @param bootstrap_addr string  Temporary address the new server started on.
+--- @param expected_uis   integer Number of UIs expected to reattach (0 = don't wait).
+function M._restart_handoff(canonical_addr, bootstrap_addr, expected_uis)
+  M._restart_canonical_hint = canonical_addr
+  local poll_ms = 50
+  local max_wait_ms = 30000


comment_data.body: nit/followup: do we need to pass this?`rebind_old_addr_after_restart` can infer that `v:servername` is the "boostrap" address, right?
nit/followup: technically we don't do this for the non-Windows case, right? though it's probably a good idea.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -38,4 +38,57 @@ function M.serverlist(opts, addrs)
   return addrs
 end
 
+-- (Windows only) Canonical --listen address persisted across restarts.
+M.restart_canonical_addr = nil ---@type string?
+
+--- (Windows only)
+--- Called on the new server via nvim_exec_lua RPC from the old server (:restart).
+--- Windows named pipes can't be rebound immediately, so the new server starts on a
+--- temporary bootstrap address and polls until the canonical address is reclaimable.
+--- @param canonical_addr string  The original --listen address to reclaim.
+--- @param bootstrap_addr string  Temporary address the new server started on.
+--- @param expected_uis   integer Number of UIs expected to reattach (0 = don't wait).
+function M.rebind_old_addr_after_restart(canonical_addr, bootstrap_addr, expected_uis)
+  M.restart_canonical_addr = canonical_addr
+  local poll_ms = 50
+  local max_wait_ms = 30000
+  local timer = assert(vim.uv.new_timer())
+
+  -- Poll until the canonical address can be reclaimed (or timeout).
+  local poll_elapsed = 0
+  timer:start(poll_ms, poll_ms, function()
+    vim.schedule(function()
+      poll_elapsed = poll_elapsed + poll_ms
+      if poll_elapsed >= max_wait_ms then
+        timer:stop()
+        timer:close()
+        return
+      end
+      if not vim.list_contains(vim.fn.serverlist(), canonical_addr) then
+        local ok = pcall(vim.fn.serverstart, canonical_addr)
+        if not ok then
+          return -- pipe still held by old server; retry next tick
+        end
+      end
+
+      -- Wait for UIs to reattach, then retire the bootstrap address.
+      local elapsed = 0
+      timer:stop()
+      timer:start(poll_ms, poll_ms, function()
+        vim.schedule(function()
+          elapsed = elapsed + poll_ms
+          local all_uis = expected_uis <= 0 or #vim.api.nvim_list_uis() >= expected_uis
+          if all_uis or elapsed >= max_wait_ms then
+            if canonical_addr ~= bootstrap_addr then
+              pcall(vim.fn.serverstop, bootstrap_addr)
+            end

comment_data.body: Yes, can omit, because new server running on bootstrap address, so we can just get it from there
sorry, what you mean by this? `rebind_old_addr_after_restart` is called from only windows path, so yeah stopping of bootstrap address happens only on restart in only windows case

+      if poll_elapsed >= max_wait_ms then

comment_data.body: oh sorry, right, we send `--listen` for the non-Windows case 👍

comment_data.body: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/39282

comment_regular_data.body:
> The new server now starts on a temporary address and receives the original address ... then retries `serverstart()` until the canonical address becomes available 

This makes sense! Can you put this outline in one place, probably in `ex_restart`, so that we have a single place where the approach can be understood.

The general approach makes sense, but hopefully most of the logic can live in Lua.

I did mention it somewhere, but downside of the approach is that on windows commands on restart `restart +cmd` that use address may take the temporary one instead of canonical one. For example:

- `:restart echo v:servername`
- `:restart lua vim.g.addr = vim.v.servername`
- `:restart lua vim.fn.jobstart({...})`
- `vim.fn.sockconnect('pipe', vim.v.servername, { rpc = true })`
Nice callout, I will document that.

repo.name: neovim

title: fix(:restart): --listen reusage on windows #35223
